name: WP-L12-jsx-strings: route hardcoded JSX/auth strings through i18n
description: Sweep hardcoded user-visible English JSX text into t() with keys added to en.json in the same PR (pnpm translate; dynamic keys via scripts/i18n-keep.ts with file:line cites). Burn down the 361-entry check-i18n-jsx-literals baseline as strings are routed — the ratchet ALREADY EXISTS; do not build new tooling (INV-1 reframed at slice). Findings: F-061 (163 multiline <Text> English sentences) · F-062 (auth screens entirely English) · F-026 (mode-switch error row literals bypass i18n) · F-069 (aggregate ~358 hardcoded strings) · INV-1 (baseline burn-down). Likely sub-slice at execution (tracker §4) — split by screen-group if >PR-sized. Full finding text: docs/audit/2026-05-29-full-audit/L-gap-delta.md (label l10n-a11y-mobile).
acceptanceCriteria: All in-scope JSX text-children literals routed through t(); en.json keys in same PR; i18n-jsx-literals baseline materially reduced, --accept used only for justified non-translatable copy; check-i18n-orphan-keys and check-i18n-jsx-literals green.
riskImpact: M / wide but mechanical — many screens touched, copy-only changes.
bodyText:
Bundle brief (WP-L12-jsx-strings)
Transcribed from the PRG-12 execution tracker §1 (_wip/l10n-a11y/execution-tracker.md @ 9570f5b63) — bundle #1 of 8. Children are absorbed provenance (lifecycle rides this parent): WI-633 / WI-634.
Bundle rationale
Single mechanical sweep over one file-surface: hardcoded user-visible English JSX text-children across apps/mobile. ~358 strings render English to every non-English locale today; the largest user-impact bundle in the slice (P1).
Strategy
Route every in-scope JSX text-children literal through t() with keys added to en.json in the same PR; run pnpm translate; dynamic keys go through scripts/i18n-keep.ts KEEP_PATTERNS with a real file:line cite. INV-1 is reframed at slice: the ratchet (scripts/check-i18n-jsx-literals.ts) ALREADY EXISTS with a 361-entry baseline — the work is burning the baseline down as strings are routed, NOT building tooling. --accept only for genuinely non-translatable copy, justified per commit. Likely sub-slice at execution (tracker §4): split by screen-group (e.g. auth+onboarding / session+home / rest) into stacked PRs or sibling WPs at the shepherd's call if >PR-sized.
Scope
In: F-061, F-062, F-026, F-069, INV-1 (baseline burn-down). Out: JSX *attribute* literals (accessibilityLabel/title/placeholder — that surface is WI-624 / WP-L12-label-prop-strings); extending the ratchet to attribute literals (candidate follow-up capture riding WP-4 review); pre-existing grandfathered copy that is genuinely non-translatable (brand tokens, code samples).
Findings
F-061 — 163 multiline <Text> children: hardcoded English sentences/labels (l10n-C1).
F-062 — Auth screens render entirely in English (l10n-C2).
F-026 — Mode-switch error row renders hardcoded English literals bypassing i18n.
F-069 — Coordinator synthesis: ~358 hardcoded English strings render English to every non-English locale.
INV-1 — Hardcoded JSX strings bypass i18n; guard exists (361-entry baseline) — scope is burn-down.
Full finding text: docs/audit/2026-05-29-full-audit/L-gap-delta.md (label l10n-a11y-mobile).
Acceptance Criteria (bundle)
All in-scope JSX text-children literals routed through t(); en.json keys land in the same PR; pnpm translate run.
scripts/i18n-jsx-literals-baseline.json materially reduced; every --accept justified in the commit message as non-translatable copy.
check-i18n-orphan-keys and check-i18n-jsx-literals green; no new hardcoded user-visible copy introduced.
Risk & blast radius
Low/mechanical — UI copy layer only, out-of-radius vs the identity rewrite; parallel-safe. Main risk is PR size (sub-slice on demand) and translation churn (pnpm translate output is generated — review for key collisions, not prose).
